title: Exact ABO/Rh Match First
description: The system shall prioritize blood units with exact ABO/Rh matches over other compatible types when matching transfusion requests.
rationale: This ensures that only valid cross-matches are considered, which is crucial for patient safety.
business_value: Improves the accuracy and safety of blood transfusions by ensuring compatibility before unit utilization.

source.description: As a blood bank technician, I need to match transfusion requests to compatible blood units, prioritizing exact ABO/Rh type matches first, then selecting units with the closest expiry date among compatible types.

acceptance_criteria: Given a transfusion request for a specific ABO/Rh type, when matching blood units are searched, then the system should first return units with exact ABO/Rh match sorted by closest expiry date.
Given a transfusion request with no exact ABO/Rh match available, when compatible types are considered, then the system should select units with the closest expiry date among compatible types.
Given a blood unit that has not passed cross-match validation, when matching units are evaluated, then that unit should be excluded from the results.
preconditions: The system has access to up-to-date blood unit inventory with ABO/Rh type, expiry date, and cross-match status.
The transfusion request includes a specific ABO/Rh type.
postconditions: Only valid cross-matched units are considered for the transfusion request.
Units are prioritized based on exact ABO/Rh match and then by the closest expiry date among compatible types.
functional_constraints: Exact matches must be given priority over other compatible types.
Cross-match validation status must be checked before considering a unit for matching.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should process transfusion requests and return matching units within a specified time frame.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available during peak blood transfusion hours to ensure timely matches are made.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to handle an increase in the number of transfusion requests and inventory size without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should be clear and user-friendly, providing information on why a match was not found or how to correct it.

edge_cases: What happens if there are no compatible blood units available?
How does the system handle simultaneous requests for the same exact ABO/Rh type?
What if a transfusion request is made before cross-match validation has been completed?
dependencies: Blood unit inventory data with ABO/Rh type, expiry date, and cross-match status
Cross-match validation system to ensure units are valid

interfaces.name: Blood Unit Inventory API
interfaces.description: The system will query this API to retrieve the current inventory of blood units.

interfaces.name: Transfusion Request Database
interfaces.description: The system will store and update transfusion requests and their matching results in this database.

test_cases.description: Exact ABO/Rh match available with multiple units, one having the closest expiry date.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should return the exact ABO/Rh match unit first and then other compatible types sorted by expiry date.

test_cases.description: No exact ABO/Rh match available, but compatible types exist with varying expiry dates.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should return the compatible type with the closest expiry date.

test_cases.description: A blood unit has not passed cross-match validation and is included in inventory.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should exclude this unit from matching results.
